## <title Chart: Daily Effluent Ammonia 2014-2018>

The y-axis range needs adjustment. There is an extreme outlier at approximately 316 mg/L in mid-2017 that is compressing the scale and making it difficult to see the typical performance. Please create two versions of this chart:

1. Keep the current chart but adjust the y-axis maximum to 10 mg/L (5 times the 90th percentile limit) to better visualize typical performance
2. Add a note in the code comments about the extreme outlier value that was excluded from the y-axis range

Keep all other aspects the same.
name: code_som_agent

content:
<coding_agent>:
```python
# filename: daily_effluent_ammonia_2014_2018_v2.py
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# Load the ETP Effluent Quality data
file_excel = "MWC_ETP_Data.xlsx"
df_effluent = pd.read_excel(file_excel, sheet_name="ETP Effluent Quality")

# Ensure Date is datetime and sort
df_effluent['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_effluent['Date'])
df_effluent = df_effluent.sort_values('Date')

# Filter data for 2014-01-01 to 2018-12-31
start_date = pd.Timestamp("2014-01-01")
end_date = pd.Timestamp("2018-12-31")
df_effluent = df_effluent[(df_effluent['Date'] >= start_date) & (df_effluent['Date'] <= end_date)]

# Discharge limits
annual_mean_limit = 0.5  # mg/L
percentile_90_limit = 2.0  # mg/L
# Set y-axis max to 10 mg/L (5 times 90th percentile limit) to exclude extreme outlier (~316 mg/L)
yaxis_max = 10

# Create line chart with markers for Daily Effluent Ammonia, adjusted y-axis max for typical performance visibility
fig = go.Figure()

fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df_effluent['Date'],
    y=df_effluent['Ammonia (mg/L)'],
    mode='lines+markers',
    name='Daily Effluent Ammonia (mg/L)',
    line=dict(color='blue'),
    marker=dict(size=6)
))

# Add horizontal line for annual mean discharge limit
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=[df_effluent['Date'].min(), df_effluent['Date'].max()],
    y=[annual_mean_limit, annual_mean_limit],
    mode='lines',
    name='Annual Mean Limit (0.5 mg/L)',
    line=dict(color='red', dash='dash')
))

# Add horizontal line for 90th percentile discharge limit
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=[df_effluent['Date'].min(), df_effluent['Date'].max()],
    y=[percentile_90_limit, percentile_90_limit],
    mode='lines',
    name='90th Percentile Limit (2.0 mg/L)',
    line=dict(color='orange', dash='dash')
))

# Layout update with limited y-axis max to better visualize typical performance
fig.update_layout(
    yaxis=dict(
        title='Ammonia Concentration (mg/L)',
        range=[0, yaxis_max],
        gridcolor='lightgrey',
        zeroline=True,
        zerolinecolor='black'
    ),
    xaxis=dict(title='Date'),
    template='plotly_white',
    font=dict(size=16),
    legend=dict(x=0.01, y=0.99),
    margin=dict(l=60, r=20, t=20, b=60),
)

# Save files
html_file = "daily_effluent_ammonia_2014_2018_v2.html"
png_file = "daily_effluent_ammonia_2014_2018_v2.png"
csv_file = "daily_effluent_ammonia_2014_2018_data.csv"  # reuse same filtered data CSV

fig.write_html(html_file, include_plotlyjs='cdn')
fig.write_image(png_file, width=1000, height=600)
df_effluent[['Date', 'Ammonia (mg/L)']].to_csv(csv_file, index=False)

print(html_file)
print(png_file)
print(csv_file)

# Note: The extreme outlier ammonia value of approximately 316 mg/L in mid-2017 is excluded from the y-axis range in this plot.
```
